Title: The bHLH transcription factor PIL1 orchestrates starch synthesis in key cereal crops

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; TaU3 promoter for CRISPR sgRNA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major caloric reserve in cereal grain; contributes to grain weight and yield
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in developing grain/endosperm; higher in 14 DAP endosperm than TaPIL1-5A/5B
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in developing grains/endosperm during grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation and increases TKW/yield
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via binding E-box motifs in target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TaAGPS1a-7A, TaGBSSI-4A, and TaBEIIb-2D; broadly increases expression of multiple starch synthesis-related enzyme genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid screening and assay; EMSA; dual-LUC transactivation assay; wheat overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; starch metabolite quantification; grain trait measurement
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaPIL1-5D was identified from a TaAGPS1a promoter Y1H screen and is highly expressed in developing wheat endosperm. 2. It binds E-box motifs and directly activates TaAGPS1a-7A, TaGBSSI-4A, and TaBEIIb-2D. 3. Overexpression increased amylose, amylopectin, total starch, grain size, TKW, and yield, whereas knockout reduced these traits.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator of grain starch synthesis in wheat endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field conditions; developing grain at 14 DAP analyzed
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth penalty reported; improved yield observed in overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to raise grain starch content, TKW, and yield; suitable for overexpression or favorable-allele deployment with TaPIL1-5B elite haplotypes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homologs OsPIL11 and ZmPIL1 show conserved starch-promoting functions in rice and maize, respectively.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major caloric reserve in cereal grain; contributes to grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates amylose, amylopectin, total starch, grain length, grain width, and TKW
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC assay; transgenic overexpression in rice; starch quantification; grain trait measurement
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPIL11 directly binds the promoters of OsAGPS1, OsBEIIb, and OsGBSSI. 2. It activates these promoters in dual-LUC assays. 3. Overexpression in Zhonghua11 increased starch content and TKW-related grain traits.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved positive regulator of grain starch synthesis in rice
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for increasing rice grain starch content and TKW via transgenic or allele-based improvement
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rice homolog of wheat TaPIL1 with conserved function.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major caloric reserve in cereal grain; contributes to kernel weight
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates amylose, amylopectin, total starch, grain length, grain width, and TKW
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC assay; transgenic overexpression in maize; starch quantification; grain trait measurement
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPIL1 directly binds promoters of ZmAGPS1, ZmBEIIb, and ZmGBSSI. 2. ZmPIL1 activates these starch-gene promoters in vivo. 3. Overexpression in KN5585 increased starch content and TKW-related kernel traits.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved positive regulator of grain starch synthesis in maize
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown transgenic plants across two seasons/locations
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ear number per plant was lower in overexpression lines, although tassel weight and kernel traits increased
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for maize kernel starch improvement, but deployment should monitor ear-number tradeoff
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Maize homolog of wheat TaPIL1 with conserved function.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Natural population of 301 accessions; RILs from Zhengnong17 × Yangbaimai
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native haplotype promoters; reporter fusions in GUS/LUC assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major caloric reserve in cereal grain; contributes to TKW
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing grain; Hap2/3 show higher grain expression than Hap1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in 14 DAP grains of Hap2/3 accessions than Hap1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elite promoter haplotypes Hap2/3 associate with higher amylose, amylopectin, total starch, and TKW; TaPIL1-5B protein enhances TaPIL1-5D activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; heterodimer-enhanced coactivation with TaPIL1-5D
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances activation of TaAGPS1a-7A, TaBEIIb-2D, and TaGBSSI-4A when coexpressed with TaPIL1-5D
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Repressed by TaBZR1.1 binding to BRRE element specifically in Hap1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural variation/haplotype analysis; GUS/LUC promoter assays; expression analysis in diverse accessions; multi-environment phenotyping; RIL validation; Y2H; LCI; BiFC; dual-LUC coexpression assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaPIL1-5B has three promoter haplotypes, with Hap2/3 showing higher promoter activity and higher starch/TKW than Hap1. 2. TaPIL1-5B interacts with TaPIL1-5D in the nucleus. 3. The TaPIL1-5B–TaPIL1-5D heterodimer enhances activation of starch synthesis genes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Allelic and protein-partner determinant modulating starch synthesis strength and TKW in wheat
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Six field environments for haplotype-trait associations
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TaPIL1-5B-Hap2/3 are elite alleles for marker-assisted selection to improve starch content and TKW
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing of coding and promoter regions in 301 wheat cultivars (no accession reported)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TaPIL1-5A and TaPIL1-5D showed no polymorphism in the surveyed accessions.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: RILs and haplotype materials derived from Zhengnong17/Yangbaimai and natural population
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S/pBI121-based effector in LUC assay; native TaPIL1-5B haplotype promoters as reporters
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major caloric reserve in cereal grain; contributes to TKW
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative upstream regulator that represses TaPIL1-5B-Hap1, thereby reducing starch synthesis potential and TKW relative to Hap2/3
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of starch biosynthesis regulator expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression via binding BRRE element in TaPIL1-5B-Hap1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses TaPIL1-5B-Hap1 expression; no binding/repression detected for Hap2 or Hap3 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMSA; Y1H; dual-LUC repression assay; haplotype association analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaBZR1.1 binds the BRRE-containing insertion present in the TaPIL1-5B-Hap1 promoter. 2. It directly represses Hap1 promoter activity but not Hap2/3. 3. This explains lower TaPIL1-5B expression and lower starch/TKW associated with Hap1.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditioning allele-specific repression of an upstream positive regulator, thereby modulating grain starch accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid TaBZR1.1-sensitive Hap1 promoter configuration; select TaPIL1-5B Hap2/3 alleles lacking repression for breeding
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of TaPIL1 module rather than directly on starch enzyme genes.
